intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent with a two-process architecture (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses consensus AI (Claude + GPT-4o) to autonomously generate, test, and ship software from TRD specifications. The system encompasses 16 TRD-specified subsystems: macOS application shell with Touch ID/Keychain/XPC (TRD-1), dual-model consensus engine with parallel generation and Claude arbitration (TRD-2), confidence-gated build pipeline with 20-pass fix loops and pr_type routing (TRD-3), multi-agent coordination via GitHub-stored build ledger (TRD-4), GitHub integration with rate limiting and CI workflow management (TRD-5), five-lens holistic code review (TRD-6), adaptive TRD development workflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes (TRD-7), full SwiftUI design system with health dashboard and remediation stream (TRD-8), self-hosted Mac CI runner infrastructure (TRD-9), FAISS-backed document store with injection defense (TRD-10), comprehensive security threat model (TRD-11), backend startup/shutdown sequence with version handshake (TRD-12), three-layer recovery and state management with build memory and build rules (TRD-13), CI false-positive hardening and conftest.py dual-layer import fix (TRD-14), operational runbook (TRD-15), testing strategy with FM taxonomy and 35-test regression contract (TRD-16), and self-healing autonomous observability and remediation loop (TRD-17). Cloud deployment APIs, Figma design-to-code pipeline, and CraftedConnect SDK (referenced in TRD-8 §12 and TRD-17 §5) will be resolved during PRD decomposition per operator direction.

Clarifications:
  - Create them as part of the PRD process using best practices

Operator on 'Should the initial build prioritize the Python backend (cons':
parallel from the start. Build the PRDs in order that allows for maximum code deployment efficiency. Ensure that each PR follows this guidance Max impl_files per PR: 8–10. Beyond that, split into multiple PRs.

Operator on 'For the CraftedConnect SDK (TRD-18 merged into TRD-17): shou':
Max acceptance criteria: 8–10. More than that is a scope problem.

Operator on 'TRD-1 §7.1 states v1 is NOT sandboxed. Should the build targ':
correct

Operator on 'The 35-test regression suite (TRD-16 §11) is specified as a ':
the are recommendations for tests, not explicit tests

prd_plan.title: Monorepo Foundation, Configuration Baseline, and Architecture Contracts
Swift macOS Shell Core: App Lifecycle, State Model, and Onboarding
Swift Security Layer: Touch ID, Session Control, and Keychain Management
XPC Bridge: Unix Socket Transport, Nonce Handshake, and Message Protocol
Backend Process Management: Launch, Health Monitoring, and Restart Policy
Python Backend Runtime: Startup Sequence, Command Router, and Shutdown Guarantees
GitHub Integration: Repository Operations, Rate Limiting, and CI Workflow Management
Document Store: FAISS-Backed Ingestion, Retrieval, and Injection Defense
Consensus Engine: Dual-Provider Parallel Generation and Claude Arbitration
Recovery, State Persistence, Build Memory, and Build Rules
Confidence-Gated Build Pipeline Orchestrator
PRD Planner, PR Planner, and Decomposition System
Per-PR Code Generation Pipeline: Repo Context, Self-Correction, and Lint Gate
Failure Handler, Fix Loop, and CI Feedback Remediation
Multi-Agent Coordination and GitHub-Stored Build Ledger
Holistic Code Review: Five-Lens Analysis, Report Generation, and Fix PRs
Adaptive TRD Development Workflow: Founder, Engineer, and Consultant Modes
SwiftUI Design System: Tokens, Components, and Reusable Primitives
Build Stream UI: Intent Bar, Stream Cards, Gate Cards, and Context Panels
Health Dashboard UI: Target Cards, Diagnosis Views, and Remediation Stream
TRD Session UI: Mode-Adaptive Views, Meeting Timer, and Deployment Visuals
Figma Import, Sketch Interpretation, and Design-to-Code Pipeline
Cross-Cutting Security Enforcement and Threat Controls
Self-Hosted macOS CI Runner Infrastructure and Swift Validation Pipeline
CI False-Positive Hardening and Import Path Resolution
Testing Strategy, Regression Contract, and FM Taxonomy Enforcement
Operational Runbook Integration and Operator Recovery Surfaces
Autonomous Observability, Detection, and Self-Healing Remediation Loop
CraftedConnect SDK: Target Registration, Health Reporting, and In-App Issue Submission
Cloud Deployment API Integration and Deployment Target Orchestration
